example.name: create_new_ui_component
example.scenario: User requests a new Badge-like component for status indicators with color variants (success, warning, error, info).
example.user_request: "Create a status badge component with success, warning, error, and info variants"

step.approach: Read components/ui/badge.tsx to understand the existing badge pattern. Check globals.css for available color tokens. Outcome: confirm cva pattern and available semantic colors.

Create the component following the shadcn/ui pattern: - Use cva() for variant definitions - Use cn() for class merging - Add data-slot="status-badge" attribute - Use design tokens (bg-primary, text-destructive, etc.) - Include dark mode variants where needed
Check that the component: - Follows the same pattern as existing badge.tsx - Uses design tokens, not hardcoded colors - Supports dark mode via CSS variables - Has proper TypeScript types

key_takeaways.takeaway: Always check existing components before creating new ones
Follow the cva + cn() + data-slot pattern consistently
Use semantic design tokens for all colors

example.name: build_responsive_page_layout
example.scenario: User wants a dashboard-style page with a sidebar that collapses on mobile and a main content area with a responsive card grid.
example.user_request: "Build a dashboard page with sidebar navigation and a card grid"

step.approach: Review existing layout.tsx and page patterns in the app/ directory. Check for existing sidebar components in components/ui/. Identify which shadcn/ui components to compose (Sheet, Card, etc.). Outcome: component inventory and layout plan.

Create the page file with: - Mobile: Sheet-based sidebar (hamburger trigger) - Desktop: persistent sidebar with flex layout - Card grid: responsive grid with breakpoint variants - Use existing Card, Sheet, and Button components

Verify: - Mobile: sidebar hidden, accessible via Sheet trigger - Tablet (md): possible 2-column card grid - Desktop (lg+): sidebar visible, 3-4 column card grid - All transitions are smooth with appropriate Tailwind transitions

key_takeaways.takeaway: Mobile-first approach: design for small screens, enhance for larger
Compose existing components (Sheet, Card) rather than building from scratch
Use responsive grid classes for card layouts

example.name: update_theme_tokens
example.scenario: User wants to adjust the primary color or add a new semantic color to the design system.
example.user_request: "Change the primary color to a blue tone"

step.approach: Read globals.css to find the current :root and .dark CSS variable blocks. Identify the --primary and --primary-foreground variables. Outcome: exact lines to modify.
Modify the oklch values for --primary in both :root and .dark blocks. Ensure the foreground color maintains sufficient contrast. Outcome: updated CSS variables.

Check that: - Both light and dark mode variables are updated - Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards - All components using bg-primary/text-primary-foreground update automatically - No hardcoded primary colors exist elsewhere

key_takeaways.takeaway: CSS variable changes propagate to all components using those tokens
Always update both :root (light) and .dark (dark) mode variables
Verify contrast accessibility after color changes
